title: palmijuncus amarus
intro: Palmijuncus amarus, commonly known as  Gynura procumbens, is a type of flowering plant native to Southeast Asia. It is traditionally used in herbal medicine due to its reported health benefits, including its ability to lower cholesterol levels, improve digestion, and reduce inflammation. The therapeutic actions of Palmijuncus amarus include anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and anti-diabetic effects, which are attributed to its bioactive constituents such as flavonoids, phenolic acids, and terpenoids. These bioactive compounds are believed to contribute to the plant's potential in managing various health conditions, including cardiovascular disease and metabolic disorders.
side_effects: Palmijuncus amarus can have side effects if used improperly, such as allergic reactions, skin irritation, and gastrointestinal issues. Ingesting large quantities of the plant's extract can cause nausea, vomiting, and diarrhea. Some users have also experienced headaches, dizziness, and fatigue after consuming Palmijuncus amarus. Furthermore, improper use of the plant may lead to liver and kidney damage in rare cases.
